steps.title: Identify the restrictive appositive
steps.content:
Ernesto Hernandez-Lopez is an appositive—a noun phrase that renames another noun.

It identifies the specific person described as “lawyer and cycling advocate.” Since this description alone doesn't uniquely identify a person, the name is essential information needed for identification, making this a restrictive appositive.

steps.title: Apply the restrictive appositive rule
steps.content:
Restrictive appositives are never set off by commas because they provide essential identifying information.

The name Ernesto Hernandez-Lopez must appear without commas before or after it.

Choice A incorrectly wraps the name in commas (non-restrictive pattern), while choices B and C place a comma on only one side—all violations of appositive punctuation.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
